title: Eastern Athabasca Uranium Research Workflow
subtitle: Multi-stage GeoMine Researcher demo: source planning, system reasoning, evidence lanes, and 3D communication
crs: NAD83 UTM Zone 13 referenced in source metadata; scene geometry is conceptual

layers.name: Modern GIS evidence plane
layers.note: Represents map-view public evidence layers: bedrock geology, lithogeochemistry, mineral occurrences, and planned survey sources.

layers.name: Athabasca Group sandstone cover
layers.note: Conceptual basin cover based on the regional Athabasca Basin unconformity uranium model.

layers.name: Unconformity horizon
layers.note: Interpretive horizon where sandstone cover overlies basement; not a mapped surface in this demo.

layers.name: Graphitic Wollaston-style basement package
layers.note: Conceptual reduced basement corridor analogous to evidence described in eastern Athabasca uranium occurrences.

structures.name: Reactivated basement fault corridor
structures.note: Research hypothesis: reactivated structures provide permeability and redox/fluid mixing sites.

structures.name: Hydrothermal fluid pathway
structures.note: Interpretive pathway for explaining how basinal fluids and reduced basement fluids could interact.

structures.name: Alteration halo corridor
structures.note: Visual lane for alteration, pathfinder geochemistry, and uncertainty, not a measured halo.

oreBodies.name: Conceptual uranium lens
oreBodies.note: Represents a hypothesis target at the structure-unconformity intersection. It is not a resource, reserve, or verified mineralized body.

drillholes.name: Historic analogue drill trace
drillholes.note: Conceptual trace used to explain how drilling could test the unconformity and basement structure.

drillholes.name: Proposed validation drill trace
drillholes.note: Represents the next-work design stage; final collar and azimuth require real GIS, geophysics, permits, and field validation.

gisPoints.name: Bedrock geology source
gisPoints.note: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas bedrock geology metadata supports regional geology framing.

gisPoints.name: Lithogeochemistry source
gisPoints.note: Athabasca lithogeochemistry metadata supports geochemical baseline and anomaly-lane planning.

gisPoints.name: Rabbit Lake analogue
gisPoints.note: Government of Saskatchewan SMDI record provides a public analogue for unconformity-related uranium system reasoning.

gisPoints.name: CDoGS planning lane
gisPoints.note: NRCan CDoGS is included as a public geochemistry source-planning lane; live retrieval is not claimed.

gisPoints.name: Field validation gate
gisPoints.note: Final stage where actual GIS, geophysics, field mapping, sampling, permitting, and professional review are required.

timeline.label: Research Question
timeline.description: Can a public-data, provenance-preserving workflow explain where an unconformity-related uranium target hypothesis would come from?

timeline.label: Source Inventory
timeline.description: Use public bedrock geology, Athabasca lithogeochemistry, CDoGS, and Saskatchewan mineral occurrence records as evidence lanes.

timeline.label: Entity Normalization
timeline.description: Normalize AOI, CRS, commodity, deposit model, dataset status, and caveats before drawing or reasoning.

timeline.label: System Architecture
timeline.description: Render sandstone cover, unconformity, graphitic basement, and reactivated fault corridors as a conceptual geologic architecture.

timeline.label: Evidence Reasoning
timeline.description: Use the 3D scene to explain why structure, redox contrast, geochemistry, and analogues are separate but related evidence lanes.

timeline.label: Hypothesis and Gaps
timeline.description: Show the target hypothesis and the missing evidence required before any technical or economic conclusion.

timeline.label: Work Program
timeline.description: Translate the visual hypothesis into next work: source retrieval, GIS validation, geophysics, field checks, sampling, and professional review.

provenance.label: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas - Lithogeochemistry of the Athabasca Basin
provenance.limitations: Metadata describes a historical sampling program and analytical package; this demo does not parse sample-level assay tables.

provenance.label: Saskatchewan GeoAtlas - Bedrock geology
provenance.limitations: Regional bedrock layer is useful for context; this demo does not import the vector dataset.

provenance.label: Government of Saskatchewan SMDI - Rabbit Lake Mine
provenance.limitations: Used as an analogue for research communication, not as evidence that the conceptual AOI contains a resource.

provenance.label: NRCan CDoGS
provenance.limitations: Included as a future geochemical survey retrieval lane; live retrieval is not claimed in this example.
